articles.title: Kalshi Rides Legal Winning Streak Into Montana
Introducing The Prediction Market Startups Database
articles.summary: We've built a comprehensive database tracking every prediction market startup, from the unicorns to the garage-stage hopefuls. This gives traders the intel they need to spot which platforms will actually survive long enough to pay out their bets.

articles.title: Betting on tomorrow: turning a prediction into a payday
articles.summary: Prediction markets are evolving from niche curiosities into legitimate investment vehicles where accurate forecasts translate directly into profits. This shift matters because it's attracting serious money and talent to an industry that could reshape how we price uncertainty and make collective decisions about everything from elections to economic policy.

articles.title: Dodgers Dominate Kalshi's NL Champion Market, But One Unlikely Team Is Gaining Ground
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Dodgers are crushing the National League Championship odds on Kalshi while some dark horse team is quietly climbing the board. This creates a classic prediction market setup where sharp money might be finding value in the longshot while casual bettors pile onto the obvious favorite.

articles.title: Prediction Market Making Is Hard
articles.summary: Market makers are struggling to price prediction markets accurately, getting burned by information asymmetries and volatile swings that don't follow traditional financial patterns. This creates huge opportunities for informed bettors to exploit mispriced contracts, but also threatens the liquidity that makes these markets function in the first place.

articles.title: ‘Cowboy’: Michael Selig, a top Trump Wall Street regulator, comes out swinging
articles.summary: Michael Selig, a key Trump-era Wall Street regulator, is making aggressive moves that signal a major shift in financial oversight policy. His confrontational approach could reshape market regulations and create new volatility in financial sector stocks, making regulatory policy bets suddenly very interesting.

articles.title: Prediction markets have exploded. What can they tell us about Oregon’s hottest primary race? - Oregon Public Broadcasting
articles.summary: Oregon Public Broadcasting is covering how prediction markets are being used to handicap the state's most competitive primary election. It signals that mainstream media outlets are finally treating prediction markets as legitimate political forecasting tools worth covering alongside traditional polling.

articles.title: Iran war puts prediction markets back in the spotlight
articles.summary: Iran's escalating tensions with Israel and the broader Middle East conflict are driving massive volume on geopolitical prediction markets as traders scramble to price war scenarios. The surge proves that when real money is on the line, prediction markets often provide clearer intelligence than traditional polling or expert analysis about how conflicts will actually unfold.

articles.title: Event Bets Pose a Problem for Wall Street Firms Looking to Trade
articles.summary: Wall Street firms want to trade prediction markets but can't figure out how to handle event-based bets that don't fit traditional financial models. This creates a massive opportunity for pure-play prediction market platforms to capture institutional money that banks are too rigid to chase.

articles.title: ‘Abhorrent’: the inside story of the Polymarket gamblers betting millions on war
articles.summary: High-stakes bettors are wagering millions on Polymarket's war-related prediction markets, drawing fierce criticism from observers who view profiting from human suffering as morally reprehensible. The controversy highlights prediction markets' biggest reputational risk: when financial incentives to aggregate information clash with public sensibilities about what should and shouldn't be monetized.

articles.title: Well-timed bets on Polymarket tied to the Iran war draw calls for investigations from lawmakers
White House staff told not to place bets on prediction markets
articles.summary: The Biden administration has banned White House staff from betting on prediction markets, apparently concerned about potential conflicts of interest or insider trading issues. This signals growing government attention on prediction markets as they gain mainstream adoption, which could lead to broader regulatory scrutiny that reshapes how these platforms operate.
